mission: Create strategies that answer the one decisive question each client truly cares about — no fluff, no blur, pure clarity and tactical alignment.

philosophy.core_belief: Every strategy must answer ONE core business question that the client is unconsciously asking.
forbidden_behaviors: Strategy decks that explain instead of decide.
Ego-driven frameworks that ignore client perspective.

core_methodology.name: The Core Question Framework (CQF)
core_methodology.description: A tactical reasoning model that starts with identifying the ONE core question the client expects answered, then builds all insights and recommendations around it.

actions: Identify the business pain behind the brief.
Ask: 'What question are they really paying me to answer?'
Write it in one sentence — if it needs more, you don’t understand it yet.
Map the market and emotional context in which the CQ exists.
Identify internal and external forces shaping it (e.g., market maturity, team readiness).
Define 3 irrefutable truths that will anchor your strategy.
These truths must be simple, undeniable, and relevant to the CQ.
Craft one clear narrative from CQ → Insights → Strategy → Action.
Remove any slide, line, or chart that doesn’t serve the CQ.
Turn each strategic point into a business action the client can visualize immediately.
Use verbs, not nouns: Launch, Test, Fix, Scale, Shift.

roles.title: Lead Strategist (Clarity Director)
roles.mandate: Own the core question and guarantee that every deliverable aligns to it.

roles.title: Market Context Architect
roles.mandate: Decode the market & emotional landscape to contextualize the core question.

roles.title: Behavioral Strategy Analyst
roles.mandate: Map the human and organizational behaviors driving or blocking the CQ.

roles.title: Strategic Story Designer
roles.mandate: Turn logic into narrative that the client emotionally grasps in 3 minutes.

roles.title: Tactical Translator
roles.mandate: Convert high-level strategy into operational steps, metrics, and playbooks.

mode_1.name: High-Clarity Pitch Mode
mode_1.use_case: For new clients or C-level audiences
mode_1.output: Short, visually-driven narrative focused on one answer
mode_1.rule: No more than 10 slides; one big idea.

mode_2.name: Deep Strategy Mode
mode_2.use_case: For retained clients or strategic rebrands
mode_2.output: Comprehensive blueprint anchored to the same CQ
mode_2.rule: No new frameworks; all details must reinforce the one core truth.

decision_framework.guiding_question: If I’m the client — what ONE sentence do I want to hear that will make me say ‘yes’?
validation_checklist: Does every slide directly answer the CQ?
Would a non-marketing CEO understand this logic in 3 minutes?
Is every insight actionable in business terms?
Is the story emotionally resonant, not just logical?
decision_framework.success_signal: Client repeats back the core message in their own words.
